channel.title: Chroniq Now: Breaking Global News and Headlines Daily
channel.description: Chroniq Now - your trusted source for breaking global news, in-depth analysis and up-to-the-minute coverage of politics, business, culture and more.

item.title: Lawmakers Push Back Against Plans to Dismantle Key U.S. Ocean Observatory Network
item.description:
A bipartisan group of senators and House Democrats are urging the National Science Foundation to halt plans to dismantle a $386 million ocean monitoring network, warning it could cripple climate research and extreme weather forecasting.

Key points:
• Lawmakers from both parties asked the National Science Foundation to suspend plans to dismantle the Ocean Observatories Initiative and conduct a full scientific review.
• The $386 million network includes more than 900 sensors that monitor ocean circulation, marine ecosystems, climate change, and extreme weather.
• Scientists say removing instruments from sites near Oregon, Washington, Alaska, North Carolina, and Greenland would create major gaps in long-term climate data.
• House Democrats argued the move may violate federal law and are seeking legislation to stop the decommissioning until further review is completed.

Read the full article: Lawmakers Push Back Against Plans to Dismantle Key U.S. Ocean Observatory Network

item.title: The "Croqueta Fee" Allegations: Did Personal Retaliation Taint an OCIF Enforcement Case?
item.description:
Inside the OCIF "Croqueta Fee" allegations involving Natalia Zequeira, Allied Fleet Services, AAA Car Rental, and Puerto Rico's financial regulator.

Read the full article: The "Croqueta Fee" Allegations: Did Personal Retaliation Taint an OCIF Enforcement Case?

item.title: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
item.description:
Driven Advisors and Administrative Services face scrutiny in a legal dispute with Bancrédito Holding Corp, owner of Bancrédito International Bank and Trust.

Read the full article: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
